input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.content: Now let me check what .NET SDKs are available:
data.reasoningText:
Now I need to:
1. Check available .NET SDKs
2. Create a benchmark project that targets both net9.0 and net10.0
3. Run BenchmarkDotNet comparing the three methods across both runtimes

Let me check what .NET SDKs are available.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet --list-sdks && echo \"---RUNTIMES---\" && dotnet --list-runtimes", "description": "Check available .NET SDKs and runtimes", "initial_wait": 10}

toolCalls.name: create

create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-d9827db12c954be2940dd7972dc56ca2/Benchmarks/Benchmarks.csproj
@@ -1,0 +1,15 @@
+<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
+  <PropertyGroup>
+    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
+    <TargetFrameworks>net9.0;net10.0</TargetFrameworks>
+    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
+    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
+  </PropertyGroup>
+  <ItemGroup>
+    <PackageReference Include="BenchmarkDotNet" Version="0.14.*" />
+  </ItemGroup>
+  <ItemGroup>
+    <ProjectReference Include="../TextAnalyzer/TextAnalyzer.csproj" />
+  </ItemGroup>
+</Project>
+

data.content: Need to fix the CoreRuntime for .NET 10. Let me update the program:
data.reasoningText:
The `CoreRuntime.Core100` isn't available in the BenchmarkDotNet version. Let me check what's available. I'll use a different approach - use `[SimpleJob]` attributes or specify runtimes differently. Let me check what BenchmarkDotNet version is available and what runtime monikers it supports.

Actually, for .NET 10, the BenchmarkDotNet API might use `CoreRuntime.Core100` or it might not exist yet. Let me try a different approach - I'll use the `MonoRuntime` or create custom runtime. Actually, I can create the runtime manually with `CoreRuntime.CreateForNewVersion("net10.0", ".NET 10.0")`.


I need to look into the proper way to configure .NET 10 as a target runtime in BenchmarkDotNet.

data.content: BenchmarkDotNet doesn't recognize .NET 10 yet. Let me switch to a simpler manual benchmark approach that runs on each runtime separately:
data.reasoningText:
The current version of BenchmarkDotNet doesn't support .NET 10 runtime moniker. I need to use a newer version or a different approach. Let me try using BenchmarkDotNet's nightly build, or use a manual approach with a simple benchmark loop instead.

Actually, let me try a different approach. I'll write a simpler benchmark without BenchmarkDotNet that runs on both runtimes separately, using Stopwatch. That's more reliable given the tooling constraints.

Or alternatively, I can try using the latest BenchmarkDotNet prerelease that might support .NET 10.

Let me check what BenchmarkDotNet versions are available that support .NET 10.
